description: If there is no such thing, then every thing is no such thing.
goal: (-> (not (exists x (App P x))) (forall x (not (App P x))))

hints.text: This is a fundamental quantifier-negation law: if nothing satisfies $P$, then everything fails to satisfy $P$. $\lnot \exists x. P(x) \to \forall x. \lnot P(x)$. You'll use `not-elim`, `exists-intro`, and `forall-intro`. Start by assuming the left side.
To prove $\forall x. \lnot P(x)$, introduce a fresh [atom|atom] $y$ with `fix`. This is the generic element we'll reason about — 'pick any $y$.'
Now prove $\lnot P(y)$ under that fresh $y$. $\lnot P(y)$ is just $P(y) \to \bot$, so assume $P(y)$.
From $P(y)$, use `exists-intro` to generalise: replace the concrete term $y$ with the [bound-variable|bound variable] $x$ to get $\exists x. P(x)$.
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens] on $\exists x. P(x) \to \bot$ (step 2) and $\exists x. P(x)$ (step 5): you get $\bot$.
Step 7 proves $\lnot P(y)$ with the fresh [atom|atom] $y$ in [context|context]. Use `forall-intro` to generalise: $\forall x. \lnot P(x)$.
[assumption|Discharge] the left side to finish the [implication|implication].
